message:
test(robot): add golden fixture for capabilities.json + PROVENANCE (u9osp)

Introduces the first frozen golden under `tests/golden/robot/` to
complete the harness landed in 09163cf8. The harness itself was
shipped without any checked-in fixtures; this commit populates the
canonical baseline so the `cass capabilities --json` contract is now
regression-protected.

Files:
- tests/golden/robot/capabilities.json.golden
  Frozen output of `cass capabilities --json` at commit HEAD, with
  `crate_version` scrubbed to `[VERSION]` by the harness so the
  fixture survives `cargo publish` bumps. The payload captures the
  full LLM-facing contract surface: `api_version=1`,
  `contract_version="1"`, the 19 supported connectors (codex,
  claude_code, gemini, clawdbot, vibe, opencode, amp, cline, aider,
  cursor, chatgpt, pi_agent, factory, openclaw, kimi, copilot,
  copilot_cli, qwen, crush), the 22 declared features
  (json_output, jsonl_output, robot_meta, time_filters, field_selection,
  content_truncation, aggregations, wildcard_fallback, timeout,
  cursor_pagination, request_id, dry_run, query_explain, view_command,
  status_command, state_command, api_version_command,
  introspect_command, export_command, expand_command, timeline_command,
  highlight_matches), and the `limits` block
  (max_agg_buckets=10, max_content_length=0, max_fields=50,
  max_limit=0).

- tests/golden/PROVENANCE.md
  Human-readable provenance document that explains the generation
  command (`cass capabilities --json` with `XDG_DATA_HOME` pinned to
  an isolated TempDir and `CASS_IGNORE_SOURCES_CONFIG=1` so no ambient
  sources leak into output), the scrubbing rules
  (crate_version → [VERSION], ISO-8601 timestamps → [TIMESTAMP],
  test-home paths → [TEST_HOME], UUIDs → [UUID]), the
  regeneration workflow (`UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --test
  golden_robot_json`), and the follow-up queue of goldens still
  needed under bead u9osp:
  - robot/health.json.golden (needs seeded data dir with stable
    fixture index so last_indexed_at / counts are scrubbable)
  - robot/models_status.json.golden (needs pinned CASS_DATA_DIR
    with known model-cache state so state / next_step /
    size_match are deterministic)
  - robot/robot_docs.json.golden (needs topic-specific fixtures
    since output is a large formatted doc string per topic)

Why this matters: without checked-in goldens, the harness at
tests/golden_robot_json.rs is structurally complete but has no
baseline to compare against, so any accidental change to the
LLM-facing capabilities contract (connector removal, feature flag
drop, limit change) would ship undetected. This commit makes the
contract a first-class regression target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@
+# Golden File Provenance
+
+Golden files under this directory freeze known-good outputs from cass
+subcommands. Each file should be human-reviewed before commit; any diff
+from a golden in CI is either a bug or an intentional schema change
+that requires re-approval.
+
+## Regeneration
+
+```bash
+# Regenerate every golden
+UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --test golden_robot_json
+
+# Regenerate a specific test
+UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --test golden_robot_json -- capabilities_json
+
+# After regeneration, review and commit
+git diff tests/golden/
+git add tests/golden/
+git commit -m "Update golden <name>: <why>"
+```
+
+## Scrubbing
+
+Dynamic values are scrubbed before golden comparison — see
+`tests/golden_robot_json.rs::scrub_robot_json` for the rule set:
+
+| Token | Replacement | Reason |
+|---|---|---|
+| `"crate_version": "x.y.z"` | `"[VERSION]"` | Survives `cargo publish` bumps |
+| ISO-8601 timestamps | `[TIMESTAMP]` | Non-deterministic |
+| Paths rooted at the isolated test HOME | `[TEST_HOME]` | Test-dir specific |
+| UUIDs | `[UUID]` | Non-deterministic |
+
+Keys are also sorted and the payload is re-indented by
+`serde_json::to_string_pretty` so whitespace / key-order drift is not
+treated as shape drift.
+
+## Files
+
+### `robot/capabilities.json.golden`
+
+Frozen output of `cass capabilities --json`. Captures the LLM-facing
+contract surface: `api_version`, `contract_version`, `features` list,
+`connectors` list, and `limits` block. `crate_version` is scrubbed.
+
+**Generated from:** cass @ commit HEAD of the authoring commit (see
+`git log tests/golden/robot/capabilities.json.golden`).
+**Command:** `cass capabilities --json` with `XDG_DATA_HOME` pinned to
+an isolated TempDir and `CASS_IGNORE_SOURCES_CONFIG=1` so no ambient
+sources leak into the output.
+
+## Follow-ups
+
+Goldens still to add under bead u9osp scope (each needs its own
+environment-scoped fixture — e.g. a TempDir with a known-empty or
+known-seeded data dir before the command can produce deterministic
+output):
+
+- `robot/health.json.golden` — needs a seeded data dir with a stable
+  fixture index so the `last_indexed_at` / counts are scrubbable.
+- `robot/models_status.json.golden` — needs a pinned `CASS_DATA_DIR`
+  with a known model-cache state (empty vs installed) so `state`,
+  `next_step`, and per-file `size_match` are deterministic.
+- `robot/robot_docs.json.golden` — needs a topic-specific fixture
+  since the output is a large formatted doc string per topic.
